mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, which forces equity risk premiums higher and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60d returns in >70% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return differential (>0.5% vs baseline) in >60% of triggered months, or the interaction term loses predictive power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect accelerating manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which immediately improves near-term earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following positive INDPRO revisions, or the autocorrelation-adjusted t-statistic falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of persistent inflation above 200.0, central banks prioritize price stability over growth, forcing rapid rate hikes that compress equity multiples and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60d returns in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200.0, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in high-inflation subsamples.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer spending and corporate profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces negative 20d returns in >60% of observations, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant when controlling for monetary policy shocks.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth falls below 0.5% quarter-over-quarter, economic momentum deteriorates sharply, triggering recession fears that elevate equity risk premiums and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60d returns in >70% of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 0.5%, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production expands faster than consumer price inflation, supply-side efficiency gains compress input costs and improve corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The supply-led margin expansion signal fails to predict positive 5d returns in >55% of cases, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.
